ontology.foodon_gap_note: Specific code for 'multigrain toast with peanut butter' not found in FoodOn; using closest parent for generic 'toast'.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code specific to multigrain peanut toast; A000E (Bread and similar products) used as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific prepared toast dish.

serving.description: 1 slice of multigrain toast with peanut butter
serving.natural_language_portion: One serving consists of a single slice of multigrain bread, toasted and generously spread with peanut butter.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — multigrain bread toasted (FDC ID: 170366) and peanut butter smooth with salt (FDC ID: 170198)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central data for multigrain bread (toasted) and smooth peanut butter with salt, weighted approximately 60% bread and 40% peanut butter by mass to reflect a typical serving composition.

gi_source.title: University of Sydney GI Database — analogous estimate for multigrain bread with peanut butter
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database entries for multigrain bread (GI ~55–65, medium) and peanut butter (GI ~15–20, low). Combined dish GI estimated at 58 (medium), reflecting the dominant carbohydrate contribution of the bread moderated by the fat and protein of peanut butter.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from processed bread and salted peanut butter
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should monitor sodium intake from this dish (~432 mg/100 g) and consider low-sodium bread and unsalted peanut butter to reduce cardiovascular risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index and carbohydrate load from multigrain bread
type2_diabetes.guidance: For Type 2 Diabetes management, limit portion size to one slice and pair with a protein-rich beverage to blunt the postprandial glycemic response from the medium-GI multigrain bread.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from peanut butter and sodium from processed bread
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Those with cardiovascular disease should choose natural peanut butter (lower saturated fat) and whole-grain low-sodium bread to limit saturated fat and sodium intake from this dish.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Multigrain Traditional Peanut Toast is a popular breakfast and snack item widely served in Singaporean kopitiams and café chains such as Toast Box. It consists of slices of multigrain bread — containing a blend of grains and seeds such as oats, sunflower seeds, and wheat — that are toasted until golden and crisp, then spread generously with smooth or crunchy peanut butter. The multigrain bread provides a slightly nutty, hearty flavour and a denser, chewier texture compared to white bread, along with meaningful dietary fibre. Peanut butter contributes a rich, creamy, and mildly sweet-savoury taste and adds protein and healthy unsaturated fats. Nutritionally, this dish delivers approximately 411 kcal per 100 g, with 17.3 g protein, 23.2 g fat, and 38.9 g carbohydrate, making it an energy-dense, satisfying meal. The glycemic index is estimated at 58 (medium), moderated by the fat and protein content of peanut butter. Clinically, sodium content warrants attention for hypertension patients, and portion control is advised for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease.
common_user_queries: multigrain bread with peanut butter carbs

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central data for multigrain bread (toasted) and smooth peanut butter with salt. No exact Singapore-specific HPB Foodo entry was identified for this combined dish. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database.

Dietary flags for Halal and Kosher are assumed based on typical generic ingredients and may vary by specific brand or preparation. chinese_traditional local name is null as the simplified form is sufficient and a confidently verified traditional variant was not available.
